blueprint_refs: definition: "Absolute/relative error between simulated and ground-truth daily radius of gyration computed from POI coordinates"

evidence.metrics: simulation_metrics does not include 'radius_of_gyration_error'; instead reports 'trip_distance_wasserstein'.

missing-radius-of-gyration-metric.error_identification: Blueprint requires radius_of_gyration_error, but evaluation outputs a different mobility-radius proxy (trip_distance_wasserstein). This makes results non-comparable to the spec and can mask spatial errors.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator implements step-distance Wasserstein (paired/marginal) rather than computing daily radius of gyration from POI coordinates, which the blueprint explicitly requires.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric.correct_approach: In Evaluator.compute_metrics, replace or add a radius-of-gyration calculation per (user, day): join each visit POI to (lat, lon) via dataset.poi_by_token, exclude visits missing coords per blueprint, compute gyration radius for gt and sim per day, then aggregate absolute/relative error across days. Keep trip_distance_wasserstein optional but do not substitute it for the required metric.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric.key_insight: Do not swap evaluation metrics for convenient proxies: implement required metrics exactly (or emit both with clear names) to avoid optimizing the wrong objective.

blueprint_refs: comparison_method: "Compute metrics on the 2021 validation split for each calibration trial"

evidence.metrics: Reported metrics are: category_share_mae, stop_count_abs_mean_error, stop_count_kl, tod_jsd_avg, topk_poi_recall, transition_divergence, trip_distance_wasserstein. Blueprint metric names/definitions differ (stop_count_mae, category_mix_jsd, time_of_day_emd, poi_topk_recall).

missing-true-blueprint-metrics-stopcount.error_identification: Even when execution succeeds, calibration/evaluation is being performed on a different metric set than the blueprint defines, which risks tuning toward the wrong behaviors and makes the outputs non-compliant.
missing-true-blueprint-metrics-stopcount.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator defines and optimizes non-blueprint metrics (category_share_mae, stop_count_kl, tod_jsd_avg) and uses 15-minute JSD over time bins instead of an EMD over minutes-of-day; also uses recall over first K simulated visits rather than the blueprint’s set-based day recall@K definition with explicit K choice.
missing-true-blueprint-metrics-stopcount.correct_approach: Align metrics to blueprint definitions: (1) rename/emit stop_count_mae as mean(|sim_count-gt_count|) (current stop_count_abs_mean_error already matches but name mismatches); (2) compute category_mix_jsd directly on fine categories or super-categories as specified (current js_divergence over fine categories exists but not surfaced as the main metric); (3) replace tod_jsd_avg with time_of_day_emd using minutes-of-day distributions (can reuse wasserstein_1d on minutes lists or hist CDF EMD); (4) implement poi_topk_recall as set-based recall@K per day using simulated POI set (not prefix-only), then average.
missing-true-blueprint-metrics-stopcount.key_insight: Calibration objectives must match the spec’s metric definitions (not just “similar” statistics), otherwise optimization will produce good-looking numbers that don’t satisfy the actual task.

blueprint_refs: execution step 5: "construct a 7-day context window ... may backfill from late 2020"

evidence.metrics: Not directly logged, but context_features uses max_backfill_days=30; blueprint allows backfill from late 2020 (no 30-day cap).

ood-context-window-backfill-violates-blu.error_identification: The OOD context rule is implemented with a hard 30-day backfill cap, which can prevent required backfill from late 2020 for early-2021 targets and reduce context usefulness.
ood-context-window-backfill-violates-blu.root_cause_analysis: MobilitySimulator._context_last_poi and context_features apply max_backfill_days=30 when the 7-day window is empty. The blueprint explicitly allows backfilling from late 2020 (potentially more than 30 days) as long as days are strictly earlier than the target day.
ood-context-window-backfill-violates-blu.correct_approach: Change backfill logic: when the 7-day window has no usable days, search backwards without a fixed 30-day limit but stop once you have context_days distinct prior days (and optionally log how far you had to backfill). Ensure the search never includes target_date or future days (already satisfied).
ood-context-window-backfill-violates-blu.key_insight: OOD context windows need deterministic, spec-compliant backfill rules; arbitrary caps create silent distribution shifts and undermine personalization.

blueprint_refs: baseline_poi_choice_model: "Given chosen category, choose POI via mixture of personal popularity and distance-decay"

evidence.metrics: topk_poi_recall=0.11156069364161895 on V_test; sample trajectories show many plausible-but-random POIs per day and frequent use of high-entropy categories like Convenience Store with many POIs.

poi-recall-extremely-low-from-unconditio.error_identification: POI overlap with ground truth is very low (recall@5 ~0.11), indicating POI selection is not sufficiently concentrated on each user’s likely POIs for the day.
poi-recall-extremely-low-from-unconditio.root_cause_analysis: In Resident.simulate_day, POI choice uses a very large candidate pool per fine category (ctx_top_fc + user_top_fc + global_top_fc + catalog_sample). For high-cardinality fine categories, even a preference-weighted sampler spreads probability mass across too many POIs, especially when context probabilities are sparse/limited. Additionally, recall is computed on simv[:k_eff] only, so later-day POIs never contribute.
poi-recall-extremely-low-from-unconditio.correct_approach: Tighten POI candidate generation and concentrate mass: (1) cap candidate pool size aggressively per step (e.g., top-N from user_top_fc + ctx_top_fc + global_top_fc, no large catalog_sample unless needed); (2) add a stronger per-user anchor/regular POI prior (e.g., include baseline.poi_pref and/or anchor_pois more often for first/last stops); (3) adjust evaluation to set-based day recall@K (blueprint) so any of the day’s K POIs can match; (4) log candidate_pool_size and entropy of POI weights per step to confirm concentration.
poi-recall-extremely-low-from-unconditio.key_insight: For POI-level recall tasks, controlling candidate set size is as important as weighting—high-cardinality categories require pruning/structuring to avoid probability dilution.

blueprint_refs: policy_form: "Sample stop_count ... perturbed by abnormal shift"

evidence.metrics: stop_count_abs_mean_error=1.75 (V_test). Calibration best theta_stop_count_multiplier≈0.889, yet MAE remains large.

stop-count-error-high-from-weak-2021-shi.error_identification: Daily stop counts are off by ~1.75 visits on average, suggesting the generator’s stop-count distribution doesn’t match 2021 well even after calibration.
stop-count-error-high-from-weak-2021-shi.root_cause_analysis: Stop counts are sampled by tilting a per-user baseline PMF that likely has limited support (only counts observed in 2019–2020). When 2021 has systematically higher/lower counts outside that support, tilting cannot reach those values. The context blending only uses avg_visits_per_day (scalar) and only 25% weight, which may be too weak to correct.
stop-count-error-high-from-weak-2021-shi.correct_approach: Expand and smooth stop-count support and strengthen 2021 adjustment: (1) build stop_count PMF with added support around observed counts (e.g., include +/-2 around observed keys with small prior mass) so tilting can move; (2) in simulate_day, condition stop count on day_type and context distribution (not just mean), e.g., derive a context PMF from window days and blend PMFs (baseline vs context) before applying theta_stop_count_multiplier; (3) log gt vs sim count histograms by user to detect support mismatch.
stop-count-error-high-from-weak-2021-shi.key_insight: If you calibrate a multiplier on a discrete outcome, ensure the base distribution has enough support; otherwise optimization can’t express the needed shift.

blueprint_refs: poi_topk_recall: "... POIs appearing in the simulated day (set-based)"

evidence.metrics: topk_poi_recall=0.11156069364161895; code uses simv[:k_eff] then set(), not a set over the whole simulated day with a defined K rule.

evaluation-recall-uses-prefix-not-day-se.error_identification: Recall@K is computed only on the first K simulated visits, which penalizes simulations where matching POIs occur later in the day and deviates from the spec’s set-based day recall concept.
evaluation-recall-uses-prefix-not-day-se.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics defines sim_set from simv[:k_eff], which is prefix-based. Blueprint defines recall based on POIs “appearing in the simulated day” (set-based) with K fixed by design (e.g., 5 or length).
evaluation-recall-uses-prefix-not-day-se.correct_approach: Compute recall using the set of POIs from the whole simulated day, but cap the set size to K via a clear rule (e.g., take top-K unique POIs by visit order, or by frequency, or simply set(simv) and define K=min(K,|sim_set|) consistently). Match blueprint language and document the K rule in outputs.
evaluation-recall-uses-prefix-not-day-se.key_insight: Metric definitions shape what the simulator learns; prefix-based recall silently encourages ordering hacks rather than true day-level overlap.

blueprint_refs: comparison_method: calibrate on 2021 validation split, report final performance on held-out 2021 test split

evidence.metrics: Code labels evaluation_results_on_validation but evaluates on splits.test_days_by_user (V_test) and calls it 'validation_test_rollout'.

missing-2021-validation-calibration-as-s.error_identification: Outputs/labels are confusing relative to blueprint: evaluation_results_on_validation is actually computed on the held-out 20% (V_test), while calibration uses V_calib. This risks misreporting and makes it hard to verify the required protocol.
missing-2021-validation-calibration-as-s.root_cause_analysis: In main(), the code calibrates on splits.calib_days_by_user then computes evaluation_results written to evaluation_results_on_validation.json using splits.test_days_by_user, while naming it validation. Blueprint expects explicit validation (for tuning) and a separate held-out test report.
missing-2021-validation-calibration-as-s.correct_approach: Rename artifacts and add the missing report(s): (1) write calibration-set metrics summaries for V_calib (optional but clarifying); (2) write a separate evaluation_results_on_test.json (or rename existing file) for V_test; (3) ensure the JSON includes exact split identifiers and counts for both splits, matching blueprint wording.
missing-2021-validation-calibration-as-s.key_insight: Strict split hygiene includes clear artifact naming—mislabeling validation vs test can invalidate the experimental protocol even if the code is correct.

blueprint_refs: calibration_loop: "Residents simulate trajectories ... outputs returned for scoring" (designed; does not require full sweep each iteration)

evidence.metrics: execution_time=247.218487s; calibration simulates 2647 user-days per iter * 15 iters (~39,705 user-days) plus test rollout.

runtime-too-slow-from-full-rollout-per-i.error_identification: Calibration is expensive (minutes) because each trial simulates the entire V_calib set; this limits calibration_iters and exploration, which likely hurts achievable metric quality.
runtime-too-slow-from-full-rollout-per-i.root_cause_analysis: RandomSearchCalibrator.fit calls simulator.rollout on all calib user-days every iteration, with no subsampling, caching reuse, or early rejection. This is algorithmically O(iters * |V_calib|).
runtime-too-slow-from-full-rollout-per-i.correct_approach: Implement staged evaluation in fit(): evaluate candidates on a deterministic subsample of user-days first (e.g., fixed 10–20%), keep top-M, then refine on full V_calib; or implement early stopping if partial objective exceeds best_obj by margin. Keep determinism by seeding the subsample selection from the calibrator seed.
runtime-too-slow-from-full-rollout-per-i.key_insight: When calibration is black-box, reduce per-trial cost with deterministic subsampling/Successive Halving; better exploration often beats minor model tweaks.
